channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as U.S. farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded:
Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.

(Image credit: Frank Morris)
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: OpenAI's Sora app may be going away, but its legacy will be the spread of AI video slop
item.description: Barely six months after its launch, OpenAI is ending an app that could generate AI video at the click of a button.
item.content:encoded: Barely six months after its launch, OpenAI is ending an app that could generate AI video at the click of a button.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in social media harms trial
item.description: The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: They gave her business a lifeline, then froze all her money
item.description: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.content:encoded: A murky corner of the financial world is now the fastest-growing source of funding for small businesses. One state, Connecticut, had given these lenders unusual power. That may be about to change.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Judge says government's Anthropic ban looks like punishment
item.description: Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."
item.content:encoded:
Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Higher gas prices put the squeeze on the people who deliver your food
item.description: The effects of the war in Iran are far-reaching, extending to drivers for DoorDash and Uber Eats. They're paying more for gas — and being squeezed by competition. DoorDash is rolling out some relief.
item.content:encoded:
The effects of the war in Iran are far-reaching, extending to drivers for DoorDash and Uber Eats. They're paying more for gas — and being squeezed by competition. DoorDash is rolling out some relief.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Trump delivers farmers another financial blow with Iran war
item.description: Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.
item.content:encoded:
Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Supreme Court declines to review press freedom case
item.description: At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.
item.content:encoded:
At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Kapasi

item.title: Voice of America staffers sue, alleging Kari Lake put on propaganda
item.description: Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.
item.content:encoded:
Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.

(Image credit: Voice of America)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Worried about a shaky stock market? This is what financial advisers suggest you do
item.description: Their answer depends on how soon you need to tap into your funds — and it might simply be "do nothing."
item.content:encoded:
Their answer depends on how soon you need to tap into your funds — and it might simply be "do nothing."

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
